metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes (real /rewind, restores files AND conversation)
daemon_capability: full (TUI /rewind forks to a new session dir, append-only tailer undisturbed — premise scoped to the recorded TUI path; the ACP inplace=True rewrite arm added in 2.19.1 is an unrecorded boundary, see #1089) + parent-link caveat; driver_capability: gap:keys

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (+ parent-link caveat)
driver_capability: gap:keys

Mistral Vibe is the FORK variant of checkpoint-rewind (like codex), but stronger: its rewind restores BOTH files and conversation, and — critically for the daemon pillar — the TUI `/rewind` path this cell records does NOT truncate or rewrite the append-only messages.jsonl in place; it forks to a brand-new session directory the FilesUnderRoot watcher mints as a fresh session row.

## Scope of the daemon premise (issue #1089)

The append-only premise above is scoped to **the TUI `/rewind` path, as recorded against vibe 2.19.0**. It is NOT a general claim about every vibe release or every rewind entry point:

- **2.19.1 added an in-place rewrite arm.** `SessionLogger._overwrite_messages_sync` is new in 2.19.1 (absent in 2.19.0 and every prior tag) — session_logger.py:273, tempfile + `os.replace` at :290, called at :411. The writer now branches: append when the message count grew AND a `last_message_fingerprint` boundary check passes; otherwise a full rewrite to a temp file + `os.replace()`.
- **The rewrite arm is gated on `inplace=True`, and the TUI does not pass it.** `RewindManager.rewind_to_message` takes `inplace` (rewind/manager.py:123-167); the TUI `/rewind` handler (cli/textual_ui/app.py:3556-3557) leaves the default `inplace=False` → still forks a new session dir → append-only preserved. **So this cell's premise holds for the path it actually exercises, on 2.19.0 and 2.19.1 alike.**
- **Only ACP passes `inplace=True`** (acp/acp_agent_loop.py:2003-2004). ACP shares the same `session_logger`/`session_dir`, so an ACP-driven rewind WOULD rewrite the tailed messages.jsonl under the watched root. That path is a deliberate, documented boundary of this cell: nothing in this repo drives vibe over ACP, so it is neither recorded here nor given its own scenario cell.
- **The live consequence is already handled in code (#1078).** An in-place rewrite that shrinks the file trips the tailer's rotation branch (`fileSize < lastOffset`, core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go:497-501), and #1078 made that branch reset the vibe parser's accumulators through the `rotationResetter` opt-in — so vibe tokens/cost no longer flatline to 0 after a rotation. Regression test: `TestTokenAccounting_ResumesAfterRotationReset` (core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/metrics_test.go). The ACP boundary is therefore an unrecorded path, not an open bug. (Rotation detection itself only catches the SHRINK case; a rewrite landing at >= lastOffset is a separate latent gap — #1104.)

`agent_cli_version: 2.19.0` is the version this cell was RECORDED against and is deliberately left as-is: it is provenance of the recording, not a claim about the current vibe release.

## agent_supports: yes

Vibe ships a real rewind affordance, reachable live in v2.19.0:
- `/rewind` slash command (cli/commands.py:154, aliases `/rewind`, description "Rewind to a previous message", handler `_start_rewind_mode`), plus an `Alt+↑↓` / `Ctrl+P/N` shortcut (cli/commands.py:243).
- It opens a `RewindApp` bottom-panel TUI picker (cli/textual_ui/widgets/rewind_app.py) that navigates the list of prior user messages and offers TWO actions: `EDIT_AND_RESTORE` (restore files + edit the message) and `EDIT_ONLY` (conversation only).
- `RewindManager.rewind_to_message(..., restore_files=...)` (core/rewind/manager.py) truncates the conversation to the chosen user message AND, when restore_files is set, writes the file snapshots captured at that checkpoint back to disk (`_restore_checkpoint`). Checkpoints are auto-created per user message (agent_loop.py:798 `create_checkpoint`).

This is `yes`, not `partial`: vibe covers BOTH halves of "files and/or conversation" (codex is `partial` because it reverts conversation only; vibe's EDIT_AND_RESTORE reverts files too).

## daemon_capability: full (+ caveat)

The Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.vibe/logs/session}` with `SessionIDFromPath` = the `<session-id>` directory (adapter.go:56, agent.go:31). The daemon tails messages.jsonl as APPEND-ONLY. The task's central risk — a rewind that TRUNCATES or REWRITES messages.jsonl would defeat the append-oriented tailer — does NOT occur on the TUI `/rewind` path this cell records (see "Scope of the daemon premise" for the ACP exception):

1. Rewind FORKS. `RewindManager.rewind_to_message` calls `_reset_session` (agent_loop.py:1848), which mints a NEW session_id (`generate_session_id`, parent = old id) and repoints the SessionLogger to a fresh directory (session_logger.py:393 `reset_session`). The truncated pre-rewind history is then re-saved into the NEW `<new_session_id>/messages.jsonl`.
2. The ORIGINAL messages.jsonl is never mutated ON THIS PATH. `persist_messages` opens the file in `"a"` (append) mode (session_logger.py:247) and `save_interaction` only appends messages BEYOND the metadata `total_messages` counter (session_logger.py:300-306) — on the TUI path it can only grow a file, never truncate/rewrite it. The pre-rewind save appends nothing (everything was already persisted turn-by-turn), so the original file lingers frozen on disk. (In 2.19.1 this became a branch rather than an invariant — the rewrite arm is ACP-only; see "Scope of the daemon premise" above.) RECORD-CORRECTED: the on-disk file does stay frozen, but the original SESSION-1 daemon ROW does not stay `ready` — it is removed (transcript_removed) as the same PID reconciles onto SESSION-2. See the RECORD-CORRECTED entry in recipe.verify.

So the daemon observes exactly the codex-fork shape: a NEW session_id directory appears under the watched root → a new session row in `ready`, and turn 3 drives a clean `ready → working → ready` on it. Every user-observable signal the scenario asserts is emitted today. Confirmed against live data: all 6 real transcripts under ~/.vibe/logs/session carry a `parent_session_id` field in meta.json (null when unforked — it would carry the old id on a fork).

### Caveat (not a bug): parent linkage label is lost

`parent_session_id` lands in the FORK's meta.json, but the vibe sidecar parser reads ONLY `environment.working_directory`, `config.active_model`, and `stats.context_tokens` (sidecar.go:55-65) — it never reads `parent_session_id`, and nothing in the adapter populates `ParentSessionID`. So the daemon surfaces the fork as an INDEPENDENT new session, not a parent-linked child. Under the caveats-over-downgrades rule this stays `full` + caveat, NOT `bug`: the scenario's spec-required observable is "a (linked) new session_id appears with a clean arc," which IS satisfied — the new row appears and transitions correctly; only the provenance LABEL is dropped. Identical shape to the codex/checkpoint-rewind sibling (codex parser skips session_meta; forked_from_id dropped).

## driver_capability: gap:keys

The recipe drives the rewind through the `RewindApp` TUI picker, which needs arrow-key navigation + action selection (rewind_app.py BINDINGS: up/down move, enter/1/2 select). The freshly scaffolded driver's semantic manifest is `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` (driver-interactive.sh:113) — the `keys` arm is present-but-stubbed (`not_implemented`), so recipe-lint flags any recipe needing it as a semantic_gap before recording. `wait_turn` is ALSO stubbed (a baseline gap the whole column shares — see mistral-vibe/session-start = gap:wait_turn). Following the sibling convention of naming the scenario-DISTINCTIVE missing primitive (session-resume = gap:resume, session-end = gap:sigkill), the distinctive gap here is `keys` (the picker). This is tooling work, not an observability limit: `record` ports the `keys` (and `wait_turn`) seam from a reference driver, then drives.

## Model / observations

Active model confirmed from the live sidecars: `mistral-medium-3.5` (config.active_model, passes NormalizeModelName unchanged), context_tokens present (e.g. 22418) → tokens_nonzero. Cost is NOT asserted — the daemon emits no per-turn cost for vibe (the JSONL and sidecar carry no cost figure).
caveats: Parent-link provenance only: meta.json carries parent_session_id on a fork, but the vibe sidecar parser reads only working_directory/active_model/context_tokens (sidecar.go:55-65) and nothing sets ParentSessionID — so the fork surfaces as an INDEPENDENT new session_id, not a parent-linked child. The new row + clean arc are observed; only the linkage LABEL is lost. Same shape as codex/checkpoint-rewind.

SETTLED BY RECORD (2026-07-07, irrlichd 0.5.5+474148d, 7/11 phases): daemon=full was PROVISIONAL on the fork's new session directory materializing promptly and the original messages.jsonl staying frozen. The recording confirms both, and corrects one inference drawn from the source: the original SESSION-1 daemon row is REMOVED (transcript_removed) as the same PID reconciles onto SESSION-2, rather than lingering in `ready`. The scenario's required observable — a new session row with a clean post-rewind arc — still holds, so the verdict stays full.

Driver double-gap: keys (RewindApp picker navigation, the distinctive gap) AND wait_turn (baseline, stubbed column-wide) both need porting before record can drive this cell. keys is named as the scenario-distinctive primitive.

ACP in-place rewrite is an UNRECORDED BOUNDARY, not a gap in the recorded path (issue #1089). vibe 2.19.1 added SessionLogger._overwrite_messages_sync (tempfile + os.replace, session_logger.py:273/:290/:411), gated on inplace=True. Only the ACP loop (acp/acp_agent_loop.py:2003-2004) passes it; the TUI /rewind this cell records leaves inplace=False (cli/textual_ui/app.py:3556-3557) and still forks, so the append-only premise holds for what was recorded. An ACP-driven rewind would rewrite the tailed messages.jsonl in place; the shrink case trips the tailer's rotation branch and #1078's rotationResetter now resets vibe's accumulators there; the narrower shape where a rewrite lands at >= the tailer's lastOffset escapes rotation detection entirely and is tracked separately as a latent gap in #1104. Nothing in this repo drives vibe over ACP, so this boundary is documented rather than covered by a scenario cell.

sources.note: RewindManager: rewind_to_message truncates the conversation to a user message, optionally restores file snapshots (_restore_checkpoint), then calls reset_session (fork). create_checkpoint per user message.
/rewind slash command registered (alias /rewind, handler _start_rewind_mode); Alt+↑↓/Ctrl+P/N shortcut at :243. Live in vibe 2.19.0.

persist_messages opens messages.jsonl in append mode; save_interaction only appends messages beyond total_messages (never truncates/rewrites); reset_session repoints to a fresh session dir. Proves the append-only tailer is undisturbed by a TUI rewind. Scoped to 2.19.0 as recorded: 2.19.1 adds _overwrite_messages_sync (:273/:290/:411) as an inplace=True-only rewrite arm — see the ACP caveat.
_reset_session mints a NEW session_id (generate_session_id) with parent_session_id = old id — vibe rewind FORKS rather than rewriting in place.
Sidecar parser reads only working_directory/active_model/context_tokens; no parent_session_id / ParentSessionID wiring → fork linkage label dropped (caveat).
Live transcript: meta.json carries parent_session_id (null here, unforked), config.active_model=mistral-medium-3.5, stats.context_tokens=22418. Confirms sidecar fields + model pin.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep" — keys and wait_turn arms present but stubbed (not_implemented). Grounds driver=gap:keys.
Template: the codex fork-variant sibling (daemon=full + linkage caveat). Vibe mirrors its daemon shape and spec phasing.

The 2.19.1 in-place rewrite is gated on inplace=True: the TUI /rewind leaves the default inplace=False (forks; append-only preserved — the path this cell records), and only the ACP loop passes inplace=True. Grounds scoping the daemon premise to the TUI path and treating ACP as an unrecorded boundary.

Fixes the live consequence of an in-place rewrite: the tailer's rotation branch now resets the vibe parser's accumulators via the rotationResetter opt-in (core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go:569-572, core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/parser.go:59-90), so vibe tokens/cost stop flatlining to 0 after a rotation. Regression test TestTokenAccounting_ResumesAfterRotationReset. Why #1089 is a fixture/assessment accuracy matter rather than an open bug.

Scoped this assessment's append-only premise to the TUI /rewind path and documented the ACP inplace=True exception as a deliberate uncovered boundary. agent_cli_version stays 2.19.0 (provenance of the recording, not a claim about the current vibe release); no re-record.

Latent follow-up split out of #1089: an in-place rewrite whose new size is >= the tailer's lastOffset escapes rotation detection (core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go:496-505), so #1078's rotationResetter never fires for it. No adapter triggers it today — claudecode's in-place rewind shrinks the file, and vibe's rewrite arm is ACP-only.

preconditions: mistral-vibe (`vibe`) v2.19.0 on PATH and authenticated
irrlichd is recording (isolated run-cell mode, or --attach against a --record daemon)
record must first port the `keys` and `wait_turn` seams into drive-mistral-vibe-interactive.sh (currently stubbed; DRIVE_ELICITS lists only send/slash/sleep)

record must confirm the exact RewindApp picker keystrokes: navigate to an earlier user message, then choose EDIT_AND_RESTORE (files+conversation) vs EDIT_ONLY — the keys below are a best-effort mirror of claudecode's /rewind picker
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (vibe mints its own session_<ts>_<hash> id; UUID arg is ignored)

Key sequence matches vibe reality: with no file changes the RewindApp shows a SINGLE option (Edit message from here), so Enter selects it → fork; vibe then pre-fills the input with the rewound message, so C-c clears it before the post-rewind send

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: one
Reply with exactly the word: two
Reply with exactly the word: three

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms + pid_discovered
SESSION-1 (session_<ts>_<hash>) appears in ready and runs two clean working→ready arcs (turns one, two)
after /rewind + Enter (single-option RewindApp), a NEW session_<...> dir (SESSION-2) + session row appears (the fork), materializing once the post-rewind turn completes

RECORD-CORRECTED: the original SESSION-1 row IS removed (transcript_removed) as the same PID reconciles onto SESSION-2 — the on-disk messages.jsonl persists, only the daemon session row is dropped (refutes the assessment’s no-removal claim)
post-rewind turn (three) drives a clean ready→working→ready arc on SESSION-2; a benign ~2s post-turn activity blip may follow
final live state is ready; observations: agent-only (model/tokens sidecar-only, unverifiable offline)
